campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to the tax rates, and the council discussed and voted on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Finance department presented the annual tax rate update
Council will vote on whether to approve the new rates
Changes will take effect in the next fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the usual first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to formally adopt it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading requirement to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not identified in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting notes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also approved several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other construction services
Approved town improvement projects
Authorized three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to approve various city projects and agreements. Main items included approving previous meeting minutes, moving forward with replacing an elevator at the Terminal, deciding how to handle homeless encampment cleanups, updating a digital advertising deal with Clear Channel, and officially recognizing a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moving forward
New process for selecting encampment cleanup services
Digital signage agreement update with Clear Channel Outdoor
Light the Night memorial event approval and funding for fallen firefighters

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear directly from community members about their concerns. The council reviewed and made funding decisions on neighborhood grant requests for the 2026-27 budget year, determining which local projects and initiatives would receive city money.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 fiscal year
Public comment period for residents to speak directly to council
Review of attendance at neighborhood meetings
Staff reports and committee updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and Commission Interviews - Council selecting members for city committees
Public Comment - Residents could speak in person or online
Study Session Format - Meeting focused on reviewing and discussing candidates

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a private negotiation meeting about buying or selling property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The city's top officials were discussing price and payment terms with the Cupertino Union School District for this piece of land. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and looked at options for getting grant funding.
keyTopics: Land deal negotiations with the school district for 10480 Finch Avenue property
Price and payment terms being worked out between city and school district
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new employment contract with the city's firefighters union for 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs on buildings near Levi's Stadium for FIFA World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters union
Withdrawn agenda item (details not specified)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve the city's spending plans for the next five years and discuss the upcoming year's budget. They also approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the council operates.
keyTopics: Approved the 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) for infrastructure and major projects
Reviewed the 2026-27 operating budget proposal
Adopted the Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council procedures and protocols handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026 to discuss routine administrative matters and a major development proposal. The main item was a public hearing about subdividing a vacant lot on Old Trace Road into nine new single-family homes plus seven smaller accessory units, which would require building a private street to access them.
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre lot at 4103 Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots
Proposal to build 9 single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Election of Council Chair and Vice Chair
Property zoned as Residential Estate (RE district)
